story.iWant: to paste text, images, and files into the input
story.soThat: I can quickly attach content from other applications
story.tasks: - Handle text paste (preserve default browser behavior) - Detect and handle image paste with thumbnail generation - Detect and handle file paste with validation - Convert to pending attachments with preview - Validate file type and size (allowlist, max 10MB) - Implement thumbnail generation for images (Canvas API) - Handle paste errors gracefully with error toasts - Add drag-and-drop support (bonus)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Pasting text works in input box (default behavior preserved) (TM §6.5)
Invalid files show error toast with specific reason (FS §3.2)
Multiple files in single paste operation supported
Image thumbnails generated for pasted images
File size validation before accepting paste (max 10MB per file)

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: ClipboardService.handlePaste(event): Process clipboard data, validate files, create pending attachments with thumbnails.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Clipboard paste operations sanitize HTML/scripts before insertion (XSS prevention). File paste validates file types against allowlist (images, PDFs only per PRD File Attachments spec). Reject executable files, scripts, and unknown MIME types.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Paste validates file types against allowlist (images: png/jpg/gif, PDFs). Paste sanitizes HTML content to prevent XSS attacks. Executable files, scripts, and unknown MIME types rejected with error message.

artifact.reason: Existing component - needs paste event handler
NEW - Display pending attachments with preview and remove button
NEW - File type and size validation logic
NEW - Image thumbnail generation using Canvas API

ecosystem.name: Desktop (Browser APIs)
ecosystem.note: Uses browser ClipboardEvent API and Canvas API (no external packages)

story-context.constraints: - **Desktop Implementation:** Uses browser ClipboardEvent API. Image thumbnails generated client-side with Canvas API. - **File Validation:** Allowlist: images (png, jpg, gif), PDFs. Max 10MB per file. Reject executables, scripts, unknown MIME types. - **XSS Prevention:** Sanitize HTML content before insertion. Strip script tags, javascript: URLs, onerror handlers. - **Multiple Files:** Loop through event.clipboardData.files, validate each, create pending attachments. - **Thumbnail Generation:** Resize to 200x200px maintaining aspect ratio. Convert to data URL for preview. - **Error Handling:** Show toast for: "File type not supported: {type}", "File too large: {size}MB (max 10MB)", "Invalid file extension: {ext}". - **Dependency on E4-S1:** NotificationService for error toasts. Dependency on E5-S1: File upload (actual upload happens in E5-S1).

interface.name: handlePaste()
interface.signature: handlePaste(event: ClipboardEvent): Promise<PasteResult> - Processes clipboard data, validates files, generates thumbnails, creates pending attachments

interface.name: validateFile()
interface.signature: validateFile(file: File): {valid: boolean, error?: string} - Checks MIME type, extension, size against allowlist/limits

interface.name: generateThumbnail()
interface.signature: generateThumbnail(file: File, maxSize?: number): Promise<string> - Generates thumbnail data URL using Canvas API, max 200x200px

interface.name: PendingAttachment
interface.signature: {id: string, file: File | Blob, filename: string, mimeType: string, sizeBytes: number, thumbnail?: string, status: 'pending' | 'uploading' | 'uploaded' | 'error'}

tests.standards: Use vitest for unit tests. Test file validator with valid/invalid files. Test thumbnail generator with various image sizes. Test MIME type and extension validation. Test paste handler with images, files, text. Test XSS sanitization with malicious HTML.

test.idea: Unit test: Paste plain text 'hello', verify inserted at cursor position in input
Unit test: Paste image (mock clipboard with image/png blob), verify thumbnail generated, PendingAttachment created
Unit test: Paste 3 files (mock clipboard.files), verify 3 PendingAttachment objects created
Unit test: Paste 15MB file, verify error toast 'File too large: 15MB (max 10MB)', attachment NOT created
Unit test: Paste .exe file, verify error toast 'File type not supported: application/x-msdownload'
Unit test: generateThumbnail(1000x1000 image, 200), verify output data URL dimensions ≤ 200x200
Security test: Paste HTML with <script>alert(1)</script>, verify script stripped before insertion
Integration test: Paste clipboard with 2 images + 1 PDF, verify 3 attachment chips rendered
